title: Open Shop Channel
requirements: A Wii with the Homebrew Channel installed
An SD card, any size up to 32GB
A Wii Remote and sensor bar
An internet connection on the Wii
warnings: The Open Shop Channel is a trusted repository, but always check app descriptions and reviews before installing.
_note: Open Shop Channel is a trusted repository for homebrew apps accessible both on and off the Wii.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
The **Open Shop Channel (OSC)** is a trusted repository of homebrew apps for the Wii — think of it as an app store for homebrew. You can browse apps on your computer at **oscwii.org** or directly on the Wii using the OSC homebrew app.

This guide installs the OSC app on your SD card so you can browse and install homebrew apps directly from your Wii. No more downloading zip files on your computer and dragging them to the SD card by hand — everything happens on the console.

Graphite handles downloading the OSC app and setting up your SD card. You just need a Wii with the Homebrew Channel already installed.

steps.title: Download the OSC app to your SD card
steps.body:
Graphite downloads the **Open Shop Channel** app and unpacks it into the right `apps` folder on your SD card.

This is the app you'll launch from the Homebrew Channel to browse and install homebrew directly on the Wii.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Move the SD card to your Wii
steps.body:
Take the SD card out of your computer and slot it into the **SD card slot on the front of the Wii**. A USB adapter won't work — it must be the built-in slot.

Make sure the console is connected to the internet. Press **I did this** once the card is in.

steps.title: Launch the Open Shop Channel
steps.body:
Power on the Wii and launch the **Homebrew Channel**.

You should see **Open Shop Channel** in the app grid. Select it with the Wii Remote.

Wait for the app to load — it fetches the latest app list from the internet when it starts.

steps.title: Browse and install apps
steps.body:
The Open Shop Channel shows a list of available homebrew apps. Use the **D-pad** to scroll.

Select an app to see its description, screenshots, and download count. Press **Install** to download and install it directly to your SD card — no computer needed.

The app appears in the Homebrew Channel the next time you launch it. Some apps need a fresh install from the **Load** button instead.

You can also visit **oscwii.org** on your computer to browse the same catalog and read reviews before installing.

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
The **Open Shop Channel** app is installed on your SD card. Whenever you want to find new homebrew, just launch it from the Homebrew Channel.

Bookmark **oscwii.org** to browse apps from your computer too — it's the same catalog.
